corrections.text: The article stated that Zig's standard library Deflate implementation 'achieves a 1.00% better compression ratio than zlib at default compression level.' This is inverted. The official Zig 0.16.0 release notes state that zlib achieves a 1.00% better compression ratio at the default compression level, meaning Zig's Deflate is currently slightly worse than zlib on compression (while being 9.5% faster on decompression).

The article described Io.Evented as pairing 'io_uring on Linux with Grand Central Dispatch on macOS for M:N threading.' This conflates three distinct backends. Io.Evented is an M:N threading (green threads / userspace stack switching) backend independent of any kernel I/O API. Io.Uring (io_uring) and Io.Dispatch (Grand Central Dispatch) are separate proof-of-concept backends listed alongside Io.Evented in the release notes; they are not components of Io.Evented.
